---
output: github_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

<!-- README.md is generated from README.Rmd. Please edit that file -->

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  fig.path = "man/figures/README-",
  out.width = "100%"
)
```

# ndexr

<!-- badges: start -->
<!-- badges: end -->

The goal of ndexr is to ...

## Installation

You can install the released version of ndexr from [CRAN](https://CRAN.R-project.org) with:

``` r
install.packages("ndexr")
```

## Example

This is a basic example which shows you how to solve a common problem:

```{r example}
library(ndexr)
con <- connection_postgres()
reddit <- connection_reddit()
```

```{r}

reddit_crawl_submissions <- function(reddit_con, subreddit = NULL, limit = 10) {
  if (!is.null(subreddit)) {
    submission_response <- iterate(reddit_con$subreddit(subreddit)$new(limit = limit))
  } else {
    submission_response <- iterate(reddit_con$front$new(limit = limit))
  } 
  
  names_nfp <- names(submission_response[[1]])
  reddit_data <- map_dfr(
    submission_response, 
    function(submission){
      map_dfr(
        names_nfp,
        function(submission_title) {
          table_obj <- tibble(
            name = submission_title,
            object = unlist(as.character(submission[submission_title]))
          )
          table_obj$unique_id <- 
            paste(
              submission$id, 
              submission$author, 
              submission$subreddit, 
              submission$created_utc, 
              sep = "-"
            )
          table_obj
        }
      )
    }
  )
}


```

```{r}

praw <- import("praw")
reddit_con <- praw$Reddit(
  client_id = Sys.getenv("REDDIT_CLIENT"),
  client_secret = Sys.getenv("REDDIT_AUTH"),
  user_agent = Sys.getenv("USER_AGENT"),
  username = Sys.getenv("USERNAME"),
  password = Sys.getenv("PASSWORD")
)

# debug(reddit_crawl_submissions)
reddit_response <- reddit_crawl_submissions(reddit_con)
```

```{r}
clean_reddit_data <- function(reddit_data = NULL) {
  
  reddit_data <- 
    reddit_data %>% 
    select(unique_id, name, object) %>% 
    pivot_wider(names_from = name, values_from = object, values_fill = NA)
  
  reddit_data <- map_df(
  reddit_data_split,
    function(submission) {
      map(submission, function(item) {
        unlist(as.character(item))
      })
    }
  )
  
  reddit_data
}

reddit_data <- clean_reddit_data(reddit_response)

# reddit_data <- split(reddit_data, reddit_data$unique_id)

# glimpse(reddit_data)
# write_csv(reddit_data, "reddit_data.csv")
reddit_data <- read_csv("reddit_data.csv")


reddit_data
```
